vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: Standard configuration change to /etc/systemd/logind.conf that terminates idle sessions; does not affect authentication mechanisms or critical services and follows STIG hardening patterns.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; May terminate legitimate long‑running sessions if timeout is set too low

execution_details.stdout:
#  This file is part of systemd.
#
#  systemd is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the
#  terms of the GNU Lesser General Public License as published by the Free
#  Software Foundation; either version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option)
#  any later version.
#
# Entries in this file show the compile time defaults. Local configuration
# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192
#StopIdleSessionSec=infinity

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly identifies and modifies the StopIdleSessionSec setting in /etc/systemd/logind.conf, and restarts systemd-logind to apply changes. This will terminate idle sessions after 5 minutes, fulfilling the vulnerability requirement. The approach is functionally adequate but could be refined for robustness.
concerns: No backup of the original configuration file is performed, which may hinder rollback if needed.
The plan uses a single sed command without verifying that the file is in a valid state before writing, potentially corrupting the file if multiple [Login] sections exist.
Systemd may cache the configuration; a daemon-reload might be preferred over a full restart to preserve other active sessions.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf before making changes (e.g., cp logind.conf{,.bak}) to allow rollback.
Use a more robust script that verifies the presence of a single [Login] section and inserts or updates the setting with a clear, idempotent sed or awk command.

After modifying the file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' followed by 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' only if the file was actually changed, or use 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' unconditionally as done here, which is acceptable for most environments.
Add a check for file permissions and ownership before attempting the sed operation to avoid permission errors.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will effectively apply the intended idle timeout setting and reload logind, but it could be refined to be more idempotent and robust.
concerns: Potential duplicate StopIdleSessionSec entries if multiple [Login] sections exist or if the sed append runs unconditionally after a match.
SED commands lack a pre-check to see if the configuration is already set correctly, which could modify unintended lines.
No explicit handling of file permissions/ownership before editing may result in permission errors on systems with tighter controls.
The plan restarts systemd-logind unconditionally, which may interrupt active sessions unnecessarily.
The backup is performed, but there is no verification that the backup was successful before proceeding.
suggested_improvements: Use a single, idempotent sed or awk command that adds or updates the StopIdleSessionSec line within the [Login] section, ensuring no duplicates.
Add a condition to perform 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' only if the file was actually modified.
Verify and preserve the file’s permissions and ownership before and after editing.
If the system has multiple [Login] sections, merge or consolidate them to avoid ambiguous settings.
Optionally, include validation that the added value ('5min') is acceptable for the system’s operational requirements.
